quick_facts.label: Current classification
quick_facts.value: Inspector of Income Tax in the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) is listed as a Group B post in the SSC Combined Graduate Level Examination (CGLE) 2026 notice. This corrects the supplied node description’s “Group C” label; SSC also issued a 2025 corrigendum changing this post’s classification from Group C to Group B.

quick_facts.label: Main entry route
quick_facts.value: The principal direct-recruitment route is SSC’s Combined Graduate Level Examination, followed by post/department preference and final allotment under the applicable SSC notice.

quick_facts.label: Education and age context
quick_facts.value: For CGLE 2026, the post was listed with an 18–30 age limit. SSC’s general qualification for posts not assigned a special qualification was a bachelor’s degree from a recognised university or equivalent; candidates should always check the notice for their own examination year and applicable relaxations.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: The CGLE 2026 notice placed the post at Pay Level 7, with the stated pay-matrix range of ₹44,900–₹1,42,400. This is basic-pay matrix context, not a guaranteed in-hand salary: allowances, deductions, place of posting and future pay revisions can change the amount received.

quick_facts.label: Geographic allocation
quick_facts.value: CBDT has previously sought region preferences from selected Income Tax Inspectors and stated that regional allocation is made on merit-cum-preference. The 2023 preference form covered 18 regions, illustrating that postings can be across India rather than confined to one city.

sections.heading: What the role does
paragraphs: An Income Tax Inspector works within the Income Tax Department under CBDT, the direct-tax board overseen by the Department of Revenue. The work involves applying tax law and handling records and information connected with direct-tax administration; exact duties depend on the charge, supervising officers and assignment.

The shorthand claim that every Inspector “conducts raids” is too broad. An Inspector can exercise specified survey-related powers when authorised: inspecting books and documents available at the survey location, checking or verifying cash, stock or valuables, and marking or copying records. Search-and-seizure operations are a separate, legally controlled enforcement function; an Inspector’s involvement, if assigned, should not be confused with an independent power to initiate or lead every search operation.
bullets: Review and verify documentary, financial and business information within assigned tax-administration work.
Conduct or assist with authorised survey and information-gathering activity, following the statutory limits and directions of the competent authority.
Maintain accuracy, confidentiality, evidence discipline and procedural compliance when handling taxpayer records.

sections.heading: How to enter through SSC CGL
paragraphs: For external candidates, the practical route is to apply for the applicable SSC Combined Graduate Level Examination and place Inspector of Income Tax/CBDT appropriately in the post-preference exercise. SSC’s CGLE 2026 notice identifies the post, CBDT, the Group B classification and the 18–30 age band; it also says final allotment depends on Tier-II performance and the candidate’s post preferences.

For CGLE 2026, Tier I was a computer-based objective examination covering General Intelligence and Reasoning, General Awareness, Quantitative Aptitude and English Comprehension. Candidates shortlisted from Tier I take Tier II; SSC states that final merit for ordinary posts is based on Tier-II Paper-I scoring, subject to qualifying the prescribed components. Examination structures, age dates, vacancy numbers, category relaxations and deadlines are cycle-specific, so the live SSC notice—not a coaching summary—should control an application.
bullets: Hold the bachelor’s qualification required under the relevant SSC notice.
Meet the post-specific age and other eligibility conditions for that cycle, including any applicable category or service relaxation.
Prepare for both computer-based tiers and submit post preferences carefully: SSC treats the selected preference as final and irreversible for allotment purposes.
After selection, complete department-side formalities, including regional preference/allocation where called for by CBDT.

sections.heading: Skills worth building
paragraphs: The role rewards careful legal and numerical work more than generic clerical speed. CBDT’s 2024 departmental Inspector paper is explicitly titled “Income Tax Law and Computation” and tests direct-tax provisions, indicating the importance of learning statutory concepts, computation logic and the disciplined interpretation of records after appointment.

For the entrance route, SSC’s CGLE structure makes quantitative aptitude, reasoning, general awareness and English comprehension directly relevant. The Tier-II scheme also includes a qualifying computer-knowledge component and data-entry speed test, so routine digital accuracy remains useful even though the Inspector post is distinct from Tax Assistant posts.
bullets: Read tax provisions and official instructions closely; distinguish survey, assessment, search and other procedures.
Perform accurate numerical checks and tax-related computations.
Analyse records, spot inconsistencies and document findings clearly.
Use computer systems carefully and protect confidential taxpayer information.
Communicate professionally in written English/Hindi as required by departmental work.

sections.heading: Workplace and posting reality
paragraphs: The service is part of CBDT’s Income Tax Department within the Department of Revenue. It combines office-based handling of tax information and records with possible authorised field activity, such as survey-related verification. The balance is not uniform across postings and should not be assumed to be either entirely desk-based or entirely field-based.

Location is a material career consideration. In CBDT’s 2023 allocation process for CGLE-2021 Inspector selectees, candidates ranked preferences among 18 departmental regions; CBDT stated that a region/state would be allotted on merit-cum-preference if a candidate did not submit preferences. That document is a historical example, not a promise that future regions, transfer policies or allocation rules will be identical.
bullets: Employer framework: Central Board of Direct Taxes, Department of Revenue, Ministry of Finance.
Likely settings: Income Tax Department offices and, where officially authorised, taxpayer business premises for statutory survey/information work.
Posting can be in different CBDT regions across India; regional allocation and later movement depend on the rules and administrative requirements in force.

sections.heading: Progression
paragraphs: Progression to Income Tax Officer (ITO) is evidenced in departmental promotion orders. For example, a July 1, 2024 order of the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax for Andhra Pradesh and Telangana records promotion of an Inspector of Income Tax to Income Tax Officer, a Group B Gazetted post in Pay Level 8. Progression is therefore possible, but timing is not automatic: it is contingent on the applicable recruitment/service rules, eligibility, seniority, vacancies, departmental promotion committee action and administrative decisions.

A CBDT draft recruitment-rules document also described the Inspector cadre as having both promotion and direct-recruitment channels, with two-thirds of vacancies proposed for promotion and one-third for direct recruitment. Because the document is expressly a draft, it is useful historical context rather than a substitute for current notified rules.
bullets: Typical next recognised promotion point evidenced by departmental orders: Income Tax Officer (ITO).
Do not rely on fixed “years to promotion” claims; vacancy position and departmental rules can materially alter progression.
Track current CBDT, Department of Personnel and Training, and regional-office orders for the rules actually applicable to a serving employee.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: As stated in SSC’s CGLE 2026 notice, Inspector of Income Tax is in Pay Level 7, whose listed matrix range is ₹44,900–₹1,42,400. The ₹44,900 figure is the entry basic pay in the Level 7 matrix, not a complete monthly salary figure.

Actual monthly gross and take-home pay vary. They depend on the employee’s matrix cell, dearness allowance and other admissible allowances, city/posting classification, pension and tax deductions, and any future changes to Central Government pay or allowance rules. Use the appointment offer, current Department of Expenditure orders and payroll office calculations for a current personal estimate.
bullets: Dated official context used here: CGLE 2026 lists Pay Level 7 at ₹44,900–₹1,42,400.
Avoid treating online “in-hand salary” estimates as universal; they usually omit location and deduction differences.
Promotion to ITO can move an employee to Pay Level 8, as shown in the cited 2024 departmental promotion order.
